title: Archive List
description: Teams need a way to review past work without digging through the archive directory by hand. The `specd archive list` command lists all archived changes from the directory configured by `storage.archivePath`, sorted by archive date descending (most recent first).
context: Teams need a way to review past work without digging through the archive directory by hand. The `specd archive list` command lists all archived changes from the directory configured by `storage.archivePath`, sorted by archive date descending (most recent first).

rules:
``` specd archive list [--format text|json|toon] [--limit ] [--page

] [--start-at ] ``` - `--format` — controls output encoding; defaults to `text` - `--limit` — maximum number of entries to return; defaults to `100` - `--page` — 1-based page number (requires `--limit`) - `--start-at` — exclusive cursor for keyset pagination; name of the change to start after `--page` and `--start-at` are mutually exclusive.

The command prints a human-readable table to stdout. Changes are sorted by archive date descending (most recently archived first).

The output has an inverse-video column header row `NAME  DATE` above the data rows. Column widths are fixed at render time, computed from the widest value across all rows for each column (global, not per-group).

At the end of the table, a summary line SHALL be printed:
`Showing <count> archived changes of <total>. Increase limit or specify another page.`
When `--format toon` is passed, the command writes the same data model as JSON encoded in Token-Oriented Object Notation (toon) to stdout.

If there are no archived changes, the command prints `no archived changes` to stdout in text mode, or `{"items":[],"meta":{"total":0,"count":0,"limit":100}}` (or equivalent empty paginated envelope) in JSON/toon mode, and exits with code 0.
- If an I/O error occurs while reading the archive directory, the command exits with code 3 and prints an `error:` message to stderr.

constraints: The archive path is determined by storage.archivePath in specd.yaml
Errors always go to stderr as plain text regardless of --format

scenarios.name: No positional arguments
then: the command proceeds without positional arguments
AND output format defaults to text

scenarios.name: Pagination flags are accepted
then: the command parses the flags and delegates them to the use case

scenarios.name: TOON format output
when: specd archive list --format toon is run
then: output is encoded in Token-Oriented Object Notation (toon)

scenarios.name: Normal text output
then: stdout contains a table with NAME and DATE columns
AND the WORKSPACE column is NOT present
AND the summary line Showing 5 archived changes of 5. is printed at the end

scenarios.name: JSON format output with metadata
given: the archive contains 125 changes and limit is 100
when: specd archive list --format json is run
then: stdout is a JSON object with items array and meta object

scenarios.name: No archived changes — text
then: AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: No archived changes — JSON
when: specd archive list --format json is run
then: AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: I/O error reading archive directory
given: the archive directory cannot be read due to a permissions error
then: AND stderr contains an error: message
